plant.title: Jade Plant
plant.toxicity_level: ⚠️ MILD TO MODERATE TOXICITY – GASTROINTESTINAL DISTRESS
plant.summary: Jade plants contain compounds that can cause vomiting, diarrhea, and lethargy in cats if ingested. While not usually life-threatening, prompt veterinary attention is recommended.
plant.what_to_do:
**Contact your veterinarian if your cat eats jade plant.**

1. Remove any plant material from the mouth.
2. Offer water to dilute.
3. Monitor for symptoms; seek veterinary care if vomiting persists.
4. Do not induce vomiting unless instructed by a professional.
sources: ASPCA Toxic and Non-Toxic Plants Database
PetMD: Jade Plant Toxicity in Cats
plant.contentHtml: ⚠️ Disclaimer: PawSafePlants provides general educational information only. This content is not veterinary advice. If your cat has been exposed to any plant, contact a licensed veterinarian or the ASPCA Animal Poison Control Center immediately. We do not guarantee the accuracy, completeness, or timeliness of this information. Use at your own risk.

Contact your veterinarian if your cat eats jade plant.

  2. Offer water to dilute.
  3. Monitor for symptoms; seek veterinary care if vomiting persists.
  4. Do not induce vomiting unless instructed by a professional.
